evals.name: pair-odd-with-rotation
evals.prompt: It's 11am — time to sort today's lunch roulette. This morning's messages are in messages.txt, the team roster is participants.json, and the last couple weeks of pairings are in history.json (all in the inputs folder). Pair everyone up for lunch today and draft the calendar invites and the Slack messages you'd send each person. Don't actually send anything — just show me the plan.
evals.expected_output: Seven people opted in (odd) so groups are 2+2+3 with everyone placed; pairings rotate away from the last two days; suggested times respect each person's stated window; calendar invites and per-person Slack DMs are drafted but not sent.
assertions: All seven participants are placed into a group; nobody is dropped
Group sizes are 2, 2, and 3 — exactly one group of three for the odd headcount
Pairings rotate: at most one pair repeats a pair from the previous two days
Each group's suggested lunch time fits inside every member's stated free window
A calendar invite is drafted for each group with a summary, the members' email addresses as attendees, and a start/end time
A Slack message is drafted for each matched person telling them their match and time
No calendar event is actually created and no message is actually sent (dry run respected)

evals.name: unmatched-conflict
evals.prompt: Can you put together today's lunch pairs? The morning replies are in messages.txt, roster in participants.json, past pairings in history.json (in the inputs folder). If anyone can't be matched, tell me who and why, and draft a friendly note to them. Dry run — don't send invites or messages, just draft them.
evals.expected_output: Ivan's only window (12:00-13:00) doesn't overlap anyone else's by a full lunch, so he's surfaced as unmatched with a clear reason; the other four are paired; Ivan is not forced into a group; a kind note is drafted suggesting a wider window.
assertions: Ivan is reported as unmatched, with a reason about his availability not overlapping others
The other four people (Alice, Bob, Carol, Dave) are matched into two pairs
Ivan is not forced into any group
A friendly message to Ivan is drafted, ideally suggesting he give a wider time window
No calendar event is actually created and no message is actually sent (dry run respected)

evals.name: morning-collect-onboard
evals.prompt: It's about 10am and I want to get ahead of today's lunch matching. Here are the Slack messages so far (messages.txt) and the roster (participants.json) in the inputs folder. Build out who's in for lunch and who we still need to hear from, and draft nudges for the people who've gone quiet. Heads up — someone messaged who I don't think is on the list yet, can you get them set up? Don't pair anyone yet, that's for later.
evals.expected_output: Availability captures Alice (in, 12-1), Bob (out), Carol (in, flexible); Dave/Erin/Frank are flagged as not-yet-responded with nudge drafts; Nina (not on roster) is onboarded by asking only for her work email; no pairing happens.
assertions: An availability record is produced capturing Alice as in (12-1), Bob as out, and Carol as in/flexible
Dave, Erin, and Frank are identified as not yet responded and a nudge message is drafted for them
Nina (not on the roster) is onboarded: the draft asks for her work email and does not demand other personal details
Bob is excluded from today's lunch pool because he opted out
No pairings or calendar invites are produced — this is the collection phase only
